# UC-13.4.5 · AI/LLM Gateway Rate-Limit Breaches, Quota Burn, Tenant-Fairness Starvation, and 429 Class Attribution (Azure OpenAI, AWS Bedrock, OpenAI, Anthropic, vLLM/Ollama, Kong/Tyk/Apigee proxies)

> **Criticality:** Critical &middot; **Difficulty:** Advanced &middot; **Pillar:** Observability &middot; **Type:** Capacity, Performance &middot; **Wave:** Walk &middot; **Status:** Verified

*We watch the doorway to the shared smart helpers like a busy bar with a fair bouncer counting drinks per guest each hour. When one crowd orders enormous pitchers back-to-back, pours pause for them so the tap does not run dry for everyone else, and we see who hit the limit before regulars wait all night.*

---

## Description

Detects AI gateway 429 responses and 403-class rate-limit denials, parses x-ratelimit remaining and reset signals, attributes breaches to tenant, application, and key fingerprint, highlights noisy-neighbor starvation and burst versus steady policy mismatch, fuses Azure OpenAI, AWS Bedrock, and self-hosted vLLM throttle evidence, and surfaces quota exhaustion risk before customers hard-fail.

## Value

Platform and product leaders receive an explainable fairness narrative that separates edge misconfiguration from true cloud quota pressure, routes incidents with owner_team and SLA class context, and gives FinOps credible lead time to raise TPM and RPM ceilings or reshuffle keys before revenue-critical assistants brown out.

## Implementation

Land ai_gateway:access, azure_openai:logs, bedrock:invoke, metrics ai_gateway_request_count, and Prometheus vLLM drop series; publish ai_gateway_tenant_inventory.csv and cmdb ai_gateway_tenant_metadata; normalize gateway, tenant, model, and key_id; save platform_uc_13_4_5_ai_gateway_rate_quota_fairness every five minutes with earliest=-65m@m; route critical and high severities to platform gateway on-call; archive weekly CSV exports with fairness annotations.

## Evidence

Saved search platform_uc_13_4_5_ai_gateway_rate_quota_fairness on a five-minute schedule; dashboard drilldowns across gateway access, Azure OpenAI logs, Bedrock invokes, metrics, and Prometheus lanes; alert routing to platform gateway on-call; weekly CSV exports archived to a restricted evidence index.

## Control test

### Positive scenario

In a lab tenant, generate sustained 429 responses on a non-production AI gateway route while forwarding ai_gateway:access, confirm platform_uc_13_4_5_ai_gateway_rate_quota_fairness returns critical or high within two five-minute windows with non-null recommended_action and rising rate_limit_pct.

### Negative scenario

After restoring normal quotas and backoff-disciplined clients, with vendor status green and gateway policies aligned to contracted_tpm, confirm severities return to info or low and the alert wrapper emits zero rows when filtering to critical and high only.

## Detailed Implementation

### Step 1 — Prerequisites

Head of Platform owns this control jointly with ML platform engineering, API gateway operators, cloud FinOps, identity and access management, site reliability teams, and observability engineers who run OpenTelemetry Collectors plus HTTP Event Collector landing zones for AI traffic. This use case is deliberately not UC-13.4.1 latency and error-rate fusion, not UC-13.4.2 token usage and dollar burn pacing, not UC-13.4.3 accelerator duty-cycle economics, and not UC-13.4.15 model drift analytics. Here the evidence story is gateway-edge rate limiting, quota class visibility, per-tenant fairness, and the mechanical semantics of HTTP 429 Too Many Requests together with vendor-specific throttling signals that do not always surface as a clean 429 in every hop. You are building a tamper-resistant ledger that answers whether one tenant, application, or API key is starving others, whether monthly token-per-minute budgets are on track to exhaust before month end, and whether your Kong, Tyk, Apigee, Azure API Management, or in-house Envoy-style proxy policies align with upstream OpenAI, Azure OpenAI Service, Amazon Bedrock, Anthropic, or self-hosted vLLM and Ollama concurrency realities.

Rate limiting is not a single knob. Token buckets refill credits at steady rates and allow bounded bursts; leaky buckets drip work at fixed throughput; some vendors implement dual counters for tokens-per-minute and requests-per-minute that refill on different clocks. OpenAI-compatible gateways often echo x-ratelimit-remaining-requests, x-ratelimit-remaining-tokens, and paired reset fields in response headers even when the status is 200, while a 429 carries Retry-After guidance that well-behaved clients honor with exponential backoff and jitter. Azure OpenAI couples deployment-level provisioned throughput with subscription-scoped quotas; raising a model deployment limit in the portal may lag behind a quota increase ticket, producing honest throttles while dashboards still show headroom elsewhere. Amazon Bedrock expresses throttling through ThrottlingException class messages and regional tokens-per-minute ceilings that differ by model family and inference profile; migrating regions resets burst history and can unexpectedly change steady-state ceilings. Anthropic documents message-window and token-window interactions where a single enormous user message consumes the window and amplifies short-term pressure even when average traffic looks calm. Self-hosted vLLM enforces max concurrent sequences and GPU memory utilization caps; Ollama inherits similar queue behavior on modest hosts. API gateways add another layer: Kong rate limiting plugins can scope by consumer, credential, header, or route; Tyk implements quotas and rate limits per policy; Apigee spike arrest policies dampen sudden bursts before they reach fragile backends. Misalignment between gateway buckets and provider buckets guarantees confusing partial failures where the edge accepts traffic the cloud refuses, or the edge refuses traffic while the cloud still shows capacity.

Bring-your-own-key versus platform-key routing changes fairness. A tenant attaching a personal free-tier key to a production route can drag an entire shared queue into aggressive upstream throttles. Weighted fair queuing and per-tenant concurrency caps at the gateway are operational necessities when multiple business units share one egress path. Observability must attribute failures to tenant, application, and key fingerprint without storing raw secrets. Hash API keys at ingest with salted transforms maintained outside interactive search for investigations. Legal reviewers should confirm whether prompts or completions may appear in access logs; when forbidden, retain headers, status classes, durations, and token tallies only.

Field normalization expectations: gateway should identify the logical edge cluster or data plane, not only a pod name. Tenant should map to a stable billing or security boundary such as subscription short code, AWS organization unit, Entra tenant identifier, or internal FinOps chargeback key. Model should reflect the upstream model or deployment after aliasing. Key identifier should be a fingerprint or consumer credential label, never a cleartext secret. x-ratelimit header names vary by case and delimiter; parsers should tolerate both X-RateLimit-Remaining-Tokens style and structured JSON access logs emitted by modern proxies. Retry-After may appear in seconds or as HTTP-date; convert to epoch seconds at ingest when possible.

Risk briefing: honest throttles during marketing events, model launches, or batch inference windows can look like incidents until annotated with change metadata. Vendor-wide brownouts produce correlated 429 storms across tenants; this control encodes provider lanes so you do not blame internal fairness when the cloud is the bottleneck. Cron jobs without backoff can amplify 429 rates through retry multiplication until budgets hollow out in minutes. Cache invalidation or feature-flag flips can create thundering herds that exhaust burst buckets even when steady-state traffic fits policy. On-call hot-fix deploys sometimes replay queued work, producing short replay storms that resemble abuse until timelines are correlated.

Differentiation recap: explicit gateway-edge rate limit and quota breach visibility with per-tenant attribution, header parsing, noisy-neighbor starvation detection, burst versus steady policy validation, and linear-style quota exhaustion forecasting. It is not UC-13.4.1 latency SLO analytics, not UC-13.4.2 token dollar burn, not UC-13.4.3 GPU duty cycles, not UC-13.4.15 drift statistics.

Index design lands complementary sourcetypes without forcing one parser to pretend it understands every vendor. Typical patterns include index=ai_gateway with sourcetype=ai_gateway:access for structured edge logs including status, route, consumer, hashed key, optional upstream_model, and response headers mirrored into fields or JSON. index=azure_openai with sourcetype=azure_openai:logs for Resource Provider or diagnostic-aligned throttling narratives. index=aws_bedrock with sourcetype=bedrock:invoke for invoke failures carrying ThrottlingException language. index=metrics with metric_name=ai_gateway_request_count for custom counters emitted by your gateway sidecars. index=prometheus or index=otel with prometheus-compatible lines for vllm_requests_dropped_total and related series from Kubernetes-hosted inference pools. index=cmdb with sourcetype=ai_gateway_tenant_metadata for support tier and cost center joins. Retention should cover at least fourteen days hot for operational triage and thirty days for quarterly governance reviews.

RBAC is mandatory. Read-only automation accounts that scrape gateway admin APIs or cloud control planes must use workload identities or short-lived tokens stored in the platform vault, not personal administrator keys on laptops. Roles that run this search need access to AI gateway indexes, metrics indexes, and CMDB inventory lanes, but must not inherit broad human-resource or payment indexes unrelated to the control.

SLO companion: mirror summarized tuples into a summary index keyed by gateway, tenant, model, and key_id for multi-week fairness reviews when executives ask whether platform keys are equitably shared across business units.

### Step 2 — Configure data collection

Stand up five complementary lanes the multisearch fans wide, plus CSV governance artifacts that keep contracted TPM and RPM limits, owner teams, and SLA class labels out of SPL literals.

Lane A, AI gateway access logs: configure Kong access log or OpenTelemetry exporter paths, Tyk gateway analytics streams, Apigee message logging, Azure API Management diagnostics, or Envoy access logs to land structured lines with HTTP status, latency, route identifiers, consumer labels, optional x-ratelimit header mirrors, Retry-After, and chosen upstream model headers. Ensure clock synchronization with NTP so reset intervals align across hosts.

Lane B, Azure OpenAI logs: stream diagnostic categories you are licensed to retain into index=azure_openai with sourcetype=azure_openai:logs, preserving subscription identifiers, deployment names, status codes, and throttling language without prompts when policy forbids raw text.

Lane C, Amazon Bedrock invoke failures: land CloudWatch or application logs with sourcetype=bedrock:invoke including errorMessage text that includes ThrottlingException or TooManyRequestsException markers, plus account and Region fields for attribution.

Lane D, Gateway throughput metrics: emit ai_gateway_request_count as a counter from your gateway data plane or sidecar into index=metrics with dimensions tenant, model, and key_id suitable for mstats aggregation in one-minute spans.

Lane E, vLLM Prometheus drops: scrape Kubernetes pods running vLLM or Ollama-compatible servers and forward vllm_requests_dropped_total or failure counters into index=prometheus or OTLP metrics indexes with namespace and pod labels mapped to tenant semantics via relabel rules.

Lookup maintenance: publish ai_gateway_tenant_inventory.csv with columns tenant, model, key_id, owner_team, business_priority, sla_class, contracted_tpm, contracted_rpm, runbook_url. Refresh when FinOps signs new contracts. Maintain ai_gateway_tenant_metadata in index=cmdb for supplemental CMDB fields such as support_tier and cost center.

Example curl helpers from hardened bastions with scoped tokens:

curl -fsS -H "Authorization: Bearer $KONG_ADMIN_TOKEN" "https://kong-admin.internal/status" | head
curl -fsS -H "Authorization: Bearer $AZURE_TOKEN" "https://management.azure.com/subscriptions/$SUB/providers/Microsoft.CognitiveServices/accounts?api-version=2023-05-01" | head
aws bedrock get-service-quota --service-code bedrock --quota-code L-1234567890abcdef --region us-east-1

props.conf guidance: set TRUNCATE safely for wide JSON, honor provider timestamps in TIME_PREFIX, and FIELDALIAS alternate header spellings to consistent field names before this search executes. transforms.conf can hash api_key fields at ingest while preserving fingerprints.

Pre-save validation: confirm each sourcetype returns rows in the last hour during business traffic, that a synthetic 429 in lab appears in gateway and provider arms within one collection interval, and that inventory lookups resolve owner_team without null spikes.

### Step 3 — Create the search and alert

Save the SPL as platform_uc_13_4_5_ai_gateway_rate_quota_fairness with a five-minute schedule during steady production, dispatch.earliest_time=-65m@m, dispatch.latest_time=now, and alert when severity is critical or high after deduplication. Throttle duplicate gateway, tenant, model, key_id tuples for twenty minutes unless severity escalates. Include recommended_action, rate_limit_pct, and distinct_keys_failing hints in pager bodies so responders open Kong manager, Tyk dashboard, Apigee trace, Azure quota blades, or Bedrock service quota consoles without guesswork.

Pipeline narrative: multisearch merges gateway access lines, Azure OpenAI logs, Bedrock throttling invokes, gateway throughput metrics, and vLLM Prometheus drops so a silent single lane cannot hide a genuine fairness crisis. bucket span=1m aligns short windows before stats computes gateway request volume, gateway rate-limit hits, provider-side hints, and header-derived remaining token minima. streamstats window=5 approximates rolling five-minute sums for rate_limit_count_5min and req_window_5m per tuple. streamstats window=5 on rate_limit_pct forms rl_pct_sustain to approximate sustained pressure. eventstats median(req_window_5m) by gateway forms env_req_baseline for noisy-neighbor context against edge clusters. eventstats sum(key_fail_flag) by gateway tenant model and minute counts distinct failing keys in that slice. join enriches ai_gateway_tenant_inventory.csv for owner_team, sla_class, contracted TPM and RPM, and runbook_url. A second join to cmdb ai_gateway_tenant_metadata adds cost-center context. case maps combined signals into severity tiers tied to recommended_action tokens. coalesce() ladders absorb field renames across vendors for gateway, tenant, model, and key identifiers.

Fenced SPL must match the spl JSON field exactly:

```spl
`comment("UC-13.4.5 AI LLM gateway rate-limit and quota breach fusion across Kong Tyk Apigee-style access logs, Azure OpenAI provider logs, AWS Bedrock throttling, gateway mstats counters, and vLLM Prometheus drops. Tunables earliest=-65m@m span=1m rl_pct_crit=0.05 exhaust_min_crit=10 keys_fail_crit=5 sustain_buckets=5")`
| multisearch
    [ search index=ai_gateway sourcetype=ai_gateway:access earliest=-65m@m latest=now
      | eval http_status=tonumber(trim(toString(coalesce(status,http_status,response_code,sc,0))))
      | eval rl403=if(http_status==403 AND (like(lower(_raw),"%rate%limit%") OR match(lower(toString(coalesce(error_code,reason,""))),"ratelimit|rate_limit|too_many_requests")),1,0)
      | eval rl_hit=if(http_status==429 OR rl403==1,1,0)
      | eval gateway=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(vhost,gateway_cluster,kong_route,tyk_api_id,apigee_proxy,forward_proxy,host,"ai_gateway_edge"))))
      | eval tenant=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(tenant_id,developer_id,subscription_id,entra_tid,org_slug,customer_id,"unknown_tenant"))))
      | eval model=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(upstream_model,azure_deployment,requested_model,model_id,engine,"unknown_model"))))
      | eval key_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(api_key_fp,consumer_key,aws_key_id,key_alias,credential_id,"unknown_key"))))
      | rex field=_raw max_match=0 "(?i)x-ratelimit-remaining-requests[\"\\s:=]+(?<rl_rem_req>\\d+)"
      | rex field=_raw max_match=0 "(?i)x-ratelimit-remaining-tokens[\"\\s:=]+(?<rl_rem_tok>\\d+)"
      | rex field=_raw max_match=0 "(?i)x-ratelimit-reset-tokens[\"\\s:=]+(?<rl_reset_tok>\\d+)"
      | rex field=_raw max_match=0 "(?i)retry-after[\"\\s:=]+(?<retry_after_sec>\\d+)"
      | eval tokens_remaining=if(rl_hit==1,tonumber(trim(toString(coalesce(rl_rem_tok,x_ratelimit_remaining_tokens,retry_after_sec,999999999)))),999999999)
      | eval requests_remaining=tonumber(trim(toString(coalesce(rl_rem_req,x_ratelimit_remaining_requests,999999999))))
      | eval reset_lag_sec=tonumber(trim(toString(coalesce(rl_reset_tok,retry_after_sec,0))))
      | eval lane="gw_access"
      | eval vol=1
      | fields _time gateway tenant model key_id rl_hit vol tokens_remaining requests_remaining reset_lag_sec lane ]
    [ search index=azure_openai sourcetype=azure_openai:logs earliest=-65m@m latest=now
      | eval http_status=tonumber(trim(toString(coalesce(status,statusCode,httpStatus,0))))
      | eval rl_hit=if(http_status==429 OR match(lower(_raw),"rate.?limit|throttl|quota"),1,0)
      | eval gateway="azure_openai_control_plane"
      | eval tenant=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(subscriptionId,tenantId,workspace,"unknown_tenant"))))
      | eval model=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(modelName,deploymentName,model,"unknown_model"))))
      | eval key_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(apiKeyHash,callerKeyId,"unknown_key"))))
      | eval tokens_remaining=if(rl_hit==1,0,999999999)
      | eval requests_remaining=999999999
      | eval reset_lag_sec=0
      | eval lane="azure_openai_logs"
      | eval vol=0
      | fields _time gateway tenant model key_id rl_hit vol tokens_remaining requests_remaining reset_lag_sec lane ]
    [ search index=aws_bedrock sourcetype=bedrock:invoke earliest=-65m@m latest=now
      | eval em=lower(toString(coalesce(errorMessage,errorCode,message,"")))
      | where match(em,"ThrottlingException|TooManyRequestsException|throttl")
      | eval rl_hit=1
      | eval gateway="aws_bedrock_invoke"
      | eval tenant=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(aws_account_id,accountId,organization_id,"unknown_tenant"))))
      | eval model=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(modelId,inferenceProfileArn,model,"unknown_model"))))
      | eval key_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(accessKeyId,iamPrincipal,"unknown_key"))))
      | eval tokens_remaining=0
      | eval requests_remaining=0
      | eval reset_lag_sec=0
      | eval lane="bedrock_throttle"
      | eval vol=0
      | fields _time gateway tenant model key_id rl_hit vol tokens_remaining requests_remaining reset_lag_sec lane ]
    [ | mstats sum(_value) AS metric_req prestats=f WHERE index=metrics metric_name=ai_gateway_request_count span=1m BY tenant, model, key_id
      | rename tenant AS tenant_raw model AS model_raw key_id AS key_raw
      | eval gateway="metrics_ai_gateway"
      | eval tenant=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(tenant_raw,"unknown_tenant"))))
      | eval model=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(model_raw,"unknown_model"))))
      | eval key_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(key_raw,"unknown_key"))))
      | eval rl_hit=0
      | eval vol=tonumber(trim(toString(coalesce(metric_req,0))))
      | eval tokens_remaining=999999999
      | eval requests_remaining=999999999
      | eval reset_lag_sec=0
      | eval lane="mstats_throughput"
      | fields _time gateway tenant model key_id rl_hit vol tokens_remaining requests_remaining reset_lag_sec lane ]
    [ search (index=prometheus OR index=metrics OR index=otel) (sourcetype=prometheus:raw OR sourcetype=statsd OR sourcetype=otel:metrics) earliest=-65m@m latest=now
      | eval mn=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(metric_name,__name__,name,""))))
      | where match(mn,"vllm_requests_dropped_total|vllm_request_failure")
      | eval rl_hit=1
      | eval gateway="vllm_prometheus"
      | eval tenant=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(kubernetes_namespace,namespace,tenant_label,"unknown_tenant"))))
      | eval model=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(model_name,model,engine,"unknown_model"))))
      | eval key_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(pod,instance,"unknown_key"))))
      | eval tokens_remaining=0
      | eval requests_remaining=0
      | eval reset_lag_sec=0
      | eval lane="vllm_drop_metric"
      | eval vol=0
      | fields _time gateway tenant model key_id rl_hit vol tokens_remaining requests_remaining reset_lag_sec lane ]
| eval gateway=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(gateway,"unknown_gateway"))))
| eval tenant=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(tenant,"unknown_tenant"))))
| eval model=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(model,"unknown_model"))))
| eval key_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(key_id,"unknown_key"))))
| bucket _time span=1m
| eval gw_vol=if(lane=="gw_access",vol,0)
| eval gw_rl=if(lane=="gw_access",rl_hit,0)
| eval ext_rl=if(lane!="gw_access",rl_hit,0)
| stats sum(gw_vol) AS gw_requests sum(gw_rl) AS gw_rl_hits sum(ext_rl) AS provider_rl_hints sum(vol) AS vol_sum min(tokens_remaining) AS tokens_remaining_min min(requests_remaining) AS requests_remaining_min max(reset_lag_sec) AS reset_lag_sec values(lane) AS lanes BY _time gateway tenant model key_id
| sort gateway tenant model key_id + _time
| streamstats window=5 global=f current=t sum(gw_rl_hits) AS rate_limit_count_5min sum(gw_requests) AS req_window_5m BY gateway tenant model key_id
| eval rate_limit_pct=if(req_window_5m>0, round(rate_limit_count_5min/req_window_5m,6), 0)
| streamstats window=2 global=f current=t last(tokens_remaining_min) AS tr_prev BY gateway tenant model key_id
| eval depletion_vel=if(isnotnull(tr_prev) AND tr_prev>tokens_remaining_min, tr_prev-tokens_remaining_min, 0)
| eval time_to_quota_exhaustion_min=if(depletion_vel>0 AND tokens_remaining_min<999999998, round(tokens_remaining_min/depletion_vel,2), if(rate_limit_pct>0.01 AND req_window_5m>0, round((1-rate_limit_pct)*30,2), 9999))
| eventstats median(req_window_5m) AS env_req_baseline BY gateway
| eval key_fail_flag=if(gw_rl_hits>0,1,0)
| eventstats sum(key_fail_flag) AS distinct_keys_failing BY gateway tenant model _time
| streamstats window=5 global=f current=t avg(rate_limit_pct) AS rl_pct_sustain BY gateway tenant model key_id
| join type=left max=0 tenant model key_id
    [| inputlookup ai_gateway_tenant_inventory.csv
     | eval tenant=lower(trim(toString(tenant)))
     | eval model=lower(trim(toString(model)))
     | eval key_id=lower(trim(toString(key_id)))
     | fields tenant model key_id owner_team business_priority sla_class contracted_tpm contracted_rpm runbook_url ]
| eval contracted_tpm=tonumber(coalesce(contracted_tpm,0))
| eval tpm_burn_slope=if(req_window_5m>0, round(req_window_5m/5,4), 0)
| eval time_to_quota_exhaustion_min=if(contracted_tpm>1000 AND tpm_burn_slope>0 AND rate_limit_pct>0.02, round(max(1,(contracted_tpm*0.08)/(tpm_burn_slope*coalesce(estimated_tok_per_req,800))),2), time_to_quota_exhaustion_min)
| join type=left max=0 tenant
    [| search index=cmdb sourcetype=ai_gateway_tenant_metadata earliest=-24h@h latest=now
     | dedup tenant
     | eval tenant=lower(trim(toString(tenant)))
     | fields tenant cmdb_cost_center support_tier]
| eval owner_team=coalesce(owner_team,"unowned")
| eval sla_class=coalesce(sla_class,"standard")
| eval severity=case(
    (coalesce(rl_pct_sustain,0)>0.05 AND coalesce(rate_limit_count_5min,0)>=3) OR coalesce(time_to_quota_exhaustion_min,9999)<10 OR coalesce(distinct_keys_failing,0)>=5, "critical",
    coalesce(rate_limit_pct,0)>0.03 OR coalesce(provider_rl_hints,0)>=2 OR coalesce(time_to_quota_exhaustion_min,9999)<30, "high",
    coalesce(rate_limit_pct,0)>0.015 OR coalesce(rate_limit_count_5min,0)>=2, "medium",
    coalesce(rate_limit_pct,0)>0.005 OR coalesce(provider_rl_hints,0)>=1, "low",
    true(), "info")
| eval recommended_action=case(
    severity=="critical" AND coalesce(distinct_keys_failing,0)>=5, "enable_weighted_fair_queue_shard_keys_increase_gateway_concurrency_caps_review_tenant_contracts",
    severity=="critical" AND coalesce(time_to_quota_exhaustion_min,9999)<10, "emergency_raise_tpm_rpm_shift_region_enable_queue_and_circuit_break_client_retries",
    match(lanes,"azure_openai"), "validate_azure_openai_deployment_quota_and_subscription_capacity_increase_PTUs_or_reroute",
    match(lanes,"bedrock"), "open_aws_support_for_bedrock_TPM_increase_or_spill_to_second_region_profile",
    match(lanes,"vllm"), "raise_vllm_max_num_seqs_and_gpu_memory_utilization_tune_batch_limits",
    severity=="high", "tighten_kong_tyk_apigee_rate_limit_plugins_add_spike_arrest_and_retry_after_jitter",
    true(), "continue_monitoring_validate_headers_and_client_backoff_policies")
| table _time gateway tenant model key_id owner_team sla_class rate_limit_count_5min rate_limit_pct tokens_remaining_min time_to_quota_exhaustion_min severity recommended_action
```

savedsearches.conf sketch:

[platform_uc_13_4_5_ai_gateway_rate_quota_fairness_alert]
cron_schedule = */5 * * * *
dispatch.earliest_time = -65m@m
dispatch.latest_time = now
enableSched = 1
action.email = 1
action.email.to = platform-ai-gateway-oncall@example.com
action.email.subject = AI gateway rate limit $result.severity$ gw=$result.gateway$ tenant=$result.tenant$
counttype = number of events
relation = greater than
quantity = 0
search = | savedsearch platform_uc_13_4_5_ai_gateway_rate_quota_fairness | where severity IN ("critical","high")

Performance: if Job Inspector shows multisearch queue time above internal objectives, materialize per-arm summaries hourly into summary indexes keyed on gateway, tenant, model, and key_id, widen alert windows on summaries, and retain this full search for investigations.

### Step 4 — Validate

Ground truth begins in gateway admin consoles and cloud control planes: compare Kong consumer rate-limit dashboards, Tyk policy usage, Apigee analytics, Azure OpenAI quota and PTU blades, AWS Service Quotas for Bedrock TPM, Anthropic console rate-limit panels, and Kubernetes Prometheus graphs for vLLM drops against Splunk rows for the same minute. When Splunk lags, verify HEC token health, OpenTelemetry exporter backpressure, and Event Hub consumer lag before muting detections.

Deep dive with exemplar request identifiers pulled from gateway logs when rate_limit_pct rises but provider lanes stay quiet, indicating a purely edge misconfiguration such as a too-tight Kong plugin. Conversely, when provider lanes spike while the gateway looks healthy, suspect bypass paths, direct-to-vendor keys, or split-horizon DNS sending some clients around the proxy.

Quota validation: in a non-production tenant, intentionally exhaust a sandbox key within approved windows, confirm rate_limit_count_5min and severity escalate, then restore quota and verify decay within two five-minute windows.

Header validation: replay captured responses with known x-ratelimit-remaining-tokens values through your parser unit tests and confirm tokens_remaining_min matches expectations after rex extractions.

Negative test: after recovery when vendor status is green, gateway policies are aligned, and fairness queues drain, confirm filtering severity to critical and high returns zero rows for six consecutive runs.

Runbook linkage: document approved load tests and chaos windows so validators do not file defects during scheduled exercises.

### Step 5 — Operationalize & Troubleshoot

Case 1 — Monthly TPM quota mostly burned in the first twelve days of the cycle: FinOps must negotiate a higher committed tier, shard tenants across multiple platform keys or regions, and enforce per-tenant pacing at the gateway until new capacity provisions.

Case 2 — One tenant submits hundred-thousand-token prompts that monopolize shared upstream windows: isolate that workload to a dedicated deployment, enable request-size guardrails, and apply weighted fair queuing so smaller interactive tenants retain minimum bandwidth.

Case 3 — Misconfigured credential labeled tier=free accidentally serves production traffic: revoke the key, rotate fingerprints, remap routes to enterprise keys, and audit CI templates that injected the wrong vault path.

Case 4 — Bedrock region migration after architecture review loses prior burst history: pre-warm traffic gradually, request quota pre-adjustment in the destination Region, and monitor ThrottlingException density during the glide path.

Case 5 — OpenAI quota increase approved in billing but provisioning lags behind ticket closure: validate organization and project limits in the vendor console, pause non-critical batch work, and communicate honest delay to product owners rather than silently retrying without backoff.

Case 6 — New feature rollout hits an unexpected requests-per-minute ceiling despite comfortable token buckets: separate chat from tool-loop agents, tune max parallel tool calls, and raise RPM thoughtfully with evidence from this detection.

Case 7 — Anthropic tier downgrade after a billing failure throttles enterprise workspaces: restore payment instruments, confirm tier reinstatement, and temporarily shed load to alternate models under change control.

Case 8 — Cron job retries without exponential backoff hollows a shared 429 budget within minutes: patch schedulers to honor Retry-After, add jitter, and cap per-minute attempts.

Case 9 — Cache invalidation event causes thundering herd through a hot route: enable stale-while-revalidate semantics, add request coalescing at the gateway, and temporarily raise burst ceilings with executive approval.

Case 10 — Bot traffic from a misconfigured Postman runner hammers a preview API: revoke runner tokens, add mTLS or IP allow lists for non-production stages, and educate partners on safe testing cadence.

Case 11 — On-call deploy hot-fix triggers a replay storm from a stuck queue consumer: drain queues safely, fix poison messages, and gate replays with rate limiters tied to this monitoring.

Case 12 — Partner SDK ignores Retry-After and multiplies failures: patch SDK, insert gateway-side smoothing, and document required client behaviors in integration contracts.

Closing checklist: five em-dash step headers are present; Step 3 fenced SPL matches the spl field exactly; multisearch lists gateway access, Azure OpenAI logs, Bedrock throttling, mstats throughput, and vLLM Prometheus arms; coalesce, streamstats, eventstats, and case appear; closing table includes gateway, tenant, model, key_id, owner_team, sla_class, rate metrics, exhaustion estimate, severity, and recommended_action joined from inventory and cmdb lanes.


## SPL

```spl
`comment("UC-13.4.5 AI LLM gateway rate-limit and quota breach fusion across Kong Tyk Apigee-style access logs, Azure OpenAI provider logs, AWS Bedrock throttling, gateway mstats counters, and vLLM Prometheus drops. Tunables earliest=-65m@m span=1m rl_pct_crit=0.05 exhaust_min_crit=10 keys_fail_crit=5 sustain_buckets=5")`
| multisearch
    [ search index=ai_gateway sourcetype=ai_gateway:access earliest=-65m@m latest=now
      | eval http_status=tonumber(trim(toString(coalesce(status,http_status,response_code,sc,0))))
      | eval rl403=if(http_status==403 AND (like(lower(_raw),"%rate%limit%") OR match(lower(toString(coalesce(error_code,reason,""))),"ratelimit|rate_limit|too_many_requests")),1,0)
      | eval rl_hit=if(http_status==429 OR rl403==1,1,0)
      | eval gateway=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(vhost,gateway_cluster,kong_route,tyk_api_id,apigee_proxy,forward_proxy,host,"ai_gateway_edge"))))
      | eval tenant=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(tenant_id,developer_id,subscription_id,entra_tid,org_slug,customer_id,"unknown_tenant"))))
      | eval model=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(upstream_model,azure_deployment,requested_model,model_id,engine,"unknown_model"))))
      | eval key_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(api_key_fp,consumer_key,aws_key_id,key_alias,credential_id,"unknown_key"))))
      | rex field=_raw max_match=0 "(?i)x-ratelimit-remaining-requests[\"\\s:=]+(?<rl_rem_req>\\d+)"
      | rex field=_raw max_match=0 "(?i)x-ratelimit-remaining-tokens[\"\\s:=]+(?<rl_rem_tok>\\d+)"
      | rex field=_raw max_match=0 "(?i)x-ratelimit-reset-tokens[\"\\s:=]+(?<rl_reset_tok>\\d+)"
      | rex field=_raw max_match=0 "(?i)retry-after[\"\\s:=]+(?<retry_after_sec>\\d+)"
      | eval tokens_remaining=if(rl_hit==1,tonumber(trim(toString(coalesce(rl_rem_tok,x_ratelimit_remaining_tokens,retry_after_sec,999999999)))),999999999)
      | eval requests_remaining=tonumber(trim(toString(coalesce(rl_rem_req,x_ratelimit_remaining_requests,999999999))))
      | eval reset_lag_sec=tonumber(trim(toString(coalesce(rl_reset_tok,retry_after_sec,0))))
      | eval lane="gw_access"
      | eval vol=1
      | fields _time gateway tenant model key_id rl_hit vol tokens_remaining requests_remaining reset_lag_sec lane ]
    [ search index=azure_openai sourcetype=azure_openai:logs earliest=-65m@m latest=now
      | eval http_status=tonumber(trim(toString(coalesce(status,statusCode,httpStatus,0))))
      | eval rl_hit=if(http_status==429 OR match(lower(_raw),"rate.?limit|throttl|quota"),1,0)
      | eval gateway="azure_openai_control_plane"
      | eval tenant=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(subscriptionId,tenantId,workspace,"unknown_tenant"))))
      | eval model=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(modelName,deploymentName,model,"unknown_model"))))
      | eval key_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(apiKeyHash,callerKeyId,"unknown_key"))))
      | eval tokens_remaining=if(rl_hit==1,0,999999999)
      | eval requests_remaining=999999999
      | eval reset_lag_sec=0
      | eval lane="azure_openai_logs"
      | eval vol=0
      | fields _time gateway tenant model key_id rl_hit vol tokens_remaining requests_remaining reset_lag_sec lane ]
    [ search index=aws_bedrock sourcetype=bedrock:invoke earliest=-65m@m latest=now
      | eval em=lower(toString(coalesce(errorMessage,errorCode,message,"")))
      | where match(em,"ThrottlingException|TooManyRequestsException|throttl")
      | eval rl_hit=1
      | eval gateway="aws_bedrock_invoke"
      | eval tenant=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(aws_account_id,accountId,organization_id,"unknown_tenant"))))
      | eval model=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(modelId,inferenceProfileArn,model,"unknown_model"))))
      | eval key_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(accessKeyId,iamPrincipal,"unknown_key"))))
      | eval tokens_remaining=0
      | eval requests_remaining=0
      | eval reset_lag_sec=0
      | eval lane="bedrock_throttle"
      | eval vol=0
      | fields _time gateway tenant model key_id rl_hit vol tokens_remaining requests_remaining reset_lag_sec lane ]
    [ | mstats sum(_value) AS metric_req prestats=f WHERE index=metrics metric_name=ai_gateway_request_count span=1m BY tenant, model, key_id
      | rename tenant AS tenant_raw model AS model_raw key_id AS key_raw
      | eval gateway="metrics_ai_gateway"
      | eval tenant=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(tenant_raw,"unknown_tenant"))))
      | eval model=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(model_raw,"unknown_model"))))
      | eval key_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(key_raw,"unknown_key"))))
      | eval rl_hit=0
      | eval vol=tonumber(trim(toString(coalesce(metric_req,0))))
      | eval tokens_remaining=999999999
      | eval requests_remaining=999999999
      | eval reset_lag_sec=0
      | eval lane="mstats_throughput"
      | fields _time gateway tenant model key_id rl_hit vol tokens_remaining requests_remaining reset_lag_sec lane ]
    [ search (index=prometheus OR index=metrics OR index=otel) (sourcetype=prometheus:raw OR sourcetype=statsd OR sourcetype=otel:metrics) earliest=-65m@m latest=now
      | eval mn=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(metric_name,__name__,name,""))))
      | where match(mn,"vllm_requests_dropped_total|vllm_request_failure")
      | eval rl_hit=1
      | eval gateway="vllm_prometheus"
      | eval tenant=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(kubernetes_namespace,namespace,tenant_label,"unknown_tenant"))))
      | eval model=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(model_name,model,engine,"unknown_model"))))
      | eval key_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(pod,instance,"unknown_key"))))
      | eval tokens_remaining=0
      | eval requests_remaining=0
      | eval reset_lag_sec=0
      | eval lane="vllm_drop_metric"
      | eval vol=0
      | fields _time gateway tenant model key_id rl_hit vol tokens_remaining requests_remaining reset_lag_sec lane ]
| eval gateway=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(gateway,"unknown_gateway"))))
| eval tenant=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(tenant,"unknown_tenant"))))
| eval model=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(model,"unknown_model"))))
| eval key_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(key_id,"unknown_key"))))
| bucket _time span=1m
| eval gw_vol=if(lane=="gw_access",vol,0)
| eval gw_rl=if(lane=="gw_access",rl_hit,0)
| eval ext_rl=if(lane!="gw_access",rl_hit,0)
| stats sum(gw_vol) AS gw_requests sum(gw_rl) AS gw_rl_hits sum(ext_rl) AS provider_rl_hints sum(vol) AS vol_sum min(tokens_remaining) AS tokens_remaining_min min(requests_remaining) AS requests_remaining_min max(reset_lag_sec) AS reset_lag_sec values(lane) AS lanes BY _time gateway tenant model key_id
| sort gateway tenant model key_id + _time
| streamstats window=5 global=f current=t sum(gw_rl_hits) AS rate_limit_count_5min sum(gw_requests) AS req_window_5m BY gateway tenant model key_id
| eval rate_limit_pct=if(req_window_5m>0, round(rate_limit_count_5min/req_window_5m,6), 0)
| streamstats window=2 global=f current=t last(tokens_remaining_min) AS tr_prev BY gateway tenant model key_id
| eval depletion_vel=if(isnotnull(tr_prev) AND tr_prev>tokens_remaining_min, tr_prev-tokens_remaining_min, 0)
| eval time_to_quota_exhaustion_min=if(depletion_vel>0 AND tokens_remaining_min<999999998, round(tokens_remaining_min/depletion_vel,2), if(rate_limit_pct>0.01 AND req_window_5m>0, round((1-rate_limit_pct)*30,2), 9999))
| eventstats median(req_window_5m) AS env_req_baseline BY gateway
| eval key_fail_flag=if(gw_rl_hits>0,1,0)
| eventstats sum(key_fail_flag) AS distinct_keys_failing BY gateway tenant model _time
| streamstats window=5 global=f current=t avg(rate_limit_pct) AS rl_pct_sustain BY gateway tenant model key_id
| join type=left max=0 tenant model key_id
    [| inputlookup ai_gateway_tenant_inventory.csv
     | eval tenant=lower(trim(toString(tenant)))
     | eval model=lower(trim(toString(model)))
     | eval key_id=lower(trim(toString(key_id)))
     | fields tenant model key_id owner_team business_priority sla_class contracted_tpm contracted_rpm runbook_url ]
| eval contracted_tpm=tonumber(coalesce(contracted_tpm,0))
| eval tpm_burn_slope=if(req_window_5m>0, round(req_window_5m/5,4), 0)
| eval time_to_quota_exhaustion_min=if(contracted_tpm>1000 AND tpm_burn_slope>0 AND rate_limit_pct>0.02, round(max(1,(contracted_tpm*0.08)/(tpm_burn_slope*coalesce(estimated_tok_per_req,800))),2), time_to_quota_exhaustion_min)
| join type=left max=0 tenant
    [| search index=cmdb sourcetype=ai_gateway_tenant_metadata earliest=-24h@h latest=now
     | dedup tenant
     | eval tenant=lower(trim(toString(tenant)))
     | fields tenant cmdb_cost_center support_tier]
| eval owner_team=coalesce(owner_team,"unowned")
| eval sla_class=coalesce(sla_class,"standard")
| eval severity=case(
    (coalesce(rl_pct_sustain,0)>0.05 AND coalesce(rate_limit_count_5min,0)>=3) OR coalesce(time_to_quota_exhaustion_min,9999)<10 OR coalesce(distinct_keys_failing,0)>=5, "critical",
    coalesce(rate_limit_pct,0)>0.03 OR coalesce(provider_rl_hints,0)>=2 OR coalesce(time_to_quota_exhaustion_min,9999)<30, "high",
    coalesce(rate_limit_pct,0)>0.015 OR coalesce(rate_limit_count_5min,0)>=2, "medium",
    coalesce(rate_limit_pct,0)>0.005 OR coalesce(provider_rl_hints,0)>=1, "low",
    true(), "info")
| eval recommended_action=case(
    severity=="critical" AND coalesce(distinct_keys_failing,0)>=5, "enable_weighted_fair_queue_shard_keys_increase_gateway_concurrency_caps_review_tenant_contracts",
    severity=="critical" AND coalesce(time_to_quota_exhaustion_min,9999)<10, "emergency_raise_tpm_rpm_shift_region_enable_queue_and_circuit_break_client_retries",
    match(lanes,"azure_openai"), "validate_azure_openai_deployment_quota_and_subscription_capacity_increase_PTUs_or_reroute",
    match(lanes,"bedrock"), "open_aws_support_for_bedrock_TPM_increase_or_spill_to_second_region_profile",
    match(lanes,"vllm"), "raise_vllm_max_num_seqs_and_gpu_memory_utilization_tune_batch_limits",
    severity=="high", "tighten_kong_tyk_apigee_rate_limit_plugins_add_spike_arrest_and_retry_after_jitter",
    true(), "continue_monitoring_validate_headers_and_client_backoff_policies")
| table _time gateway tenant model key_id owner_team sla_class rate_limit_count_5min rate_limit_pct tokens_remaining_min time_to_quota_exhaustion_min severity recommended_action
```

## CIM SPL

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t latest(Web.status) AS web_status latest(Web.http_method) AS http_method FROM datamodel=Web WHERE nodename=Web Web.url=*completion* OR Web.url=*chat* OR Web.url=*invoke* earliest=-24h@h latest=now BY Web.src Web.dest
| join type=left max=0 Web.src [| tstats summariesonly=t latest(Performance.cpu_pct) AS cpu_pct FROM datamodel=Performance WHERE nodename=Performance earliest=-24h@h latest=now BY Performance.host | rename Performance.host AS Web.src]
| where web_status>=400
| table Web.src Web.dest http_method web_status cpu_pct
```

## Visualization

Severity-colored table by gateway, tenant, model, and key_id with drilldowns to raw multisearch lanes, header-derived remaining token minima, inventory owner_team, and cmdb support_tier; timeline of rate_limit_pct and rl_pct_sustain; single-value tiles for distinct_keys_failing and time_to_quota_exhaustion_min.

## Known False Positives

Planned load tests and partner certification windows routinely generate intentional 429 storms; require change_calendar correlation and test headers before paging executives. Vendor-announced regional throttling or maintenance advisories can elevate provider_rl_hints across tenants without implying internal misconfiguration; cross-check public status pages. Scheduled batch evaluation jobs that fan out thousands of short requests may trip RPM ceilings while TPM remains comfortable; annotate batch lanes and compare against contracted_rpm from inventory. Partner integration tests reusing production routes without isolated keys can look like noisy neighbors until routes are segregated. Controlled chaos engineering exercises that validate backoff behavior may temporarily raise rate_limit_pct; gate alerts with experiment metadata. Marketing splash events with pre-approved traffic spikes can exceed historical baselines; FinOps should pre-raise quotas and tag events. Certificate or mesh upgrades that force client retries can inflate gateway volumes without true quota exhaustion; inspect retry-after patterns and TLS error adjacency. Stale inventory rows that under-report contracted_tpm can make time_to_quota_exhaustion_min look artificially dire; refresh lookups weekly. Duplicate log shipping from active-active gateways can double-count requests until dedupe summaries land; monitor source host cardinality. Brief Apigee spike-arrest dampening after configuration pushes can look like starvation until policies stabilize; compare against deployment timelines.

## References

- [OpenAI Platform — Rate limits](https://platform.openai.com/docs/guides/rate-limits)
- [Microsoft Learn — Azure OpenAI quotas and limits](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/ai-services/openai/quotas-limits)
- [AWS Documentation — Amazon Bedrock quotas](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/quotas.html)
- [Anthropic — Rate limits](https://docs.anthropic.com/en/api/rate-limits)
- [Kong — Rate Limiting plugin](https://docs.konghq.com/hub/kong-inc/rate-limiting/)
- [vLLM — Engine configuration](https://docs.vllm.ai/en/latest/configuration/engine_args.html)
- [Cloudflare Support — Error 429](https://developers.cloudflare.com/support/troubleshooting/http-status-codes/4xx-client-error/error-429/)
- [RFC 6585 — Additional HTTP Status Codes](https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc6585)
